perturbation.original_text: 1.6 HDI shall have the Site fully operational and accessible on demand by users of the Internet no later than sixty (60) days from the date this Agreement has been executed by both Parties. In the event that HDI fails to have the Site fully operational within seventy (70) days from the date of this Agreement, the Company shall have the right to terminate this Agreement without penalty.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 HDI shall have the Site fully operational and accessible on demand by users of the Internet. In the event that HDI fails to have the Site fully operational, the Company shall have the right to terminate this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific deadlines (60 and 70 days) for the Site to be operational, the contract now lacks a clear timeframe for acceptable delays. The removal of the 'without penalty' clause introduces uncertainty about potential financial repercussions of termination. This contradicts Section 4.1, which outlines the term and termination conditions including the Termination Date, and introduces ambiguity as the criteria for termination are no longer clearly defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Term; Termination. This Agreement shall be effective as of that date (the "Effective Date") the Site becomes fully operational as set forth in writing and executed by both Parties and shall continue for a period of one (1) year from the Effective Date. This Agreement shall be automatically renewed for an additional one year period on each anniversary of the Effective Date, unless terminated by either Party hereto upon ninety (90) days written notice to the other. Such notice shall specify the date on which this Agreement is to be terminated (the "Termination Date").
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a clear 60/70 day timeframe for the site to become operational. The changed text removes these deadlines, creating ambiguity and contradicting Section 4.1. Section 4.1 defines the 'Effective Date' as the date the site becomes fully operational 'as set forth in writing and executed by both Parties'. Without a clearly defined timeframe (the 60/70 days that was in the original text), it is unclear when the 'Effective Date' occurs, leading to a direct contradiction, affecting termination and renewal.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 The Company shall pay HDI thirty percent (3016) of the Net Sales in excess of Eleven Thousand Dollars ($11,000) per calendar month. "Net Sales" shall mean all revenues from the sale of Deerskin Products on the Site including shipping and handling charges, minus refunds and exchanges.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 The Company shall pay HDI a percentage of the Net Sales. "Net Sales" shall mean all revenues from the sale of Deerskin Products on the Site.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of the specific percentage (thirty percent (3016)) and the excess amount (Eleven Thousand Dollars ($11,000) per calendar month) creates a significant ambiguity regarding the compensation structure. By removing "including shipping and handling charges, minus refunds and exchanges", there is no longer a clear definition of how 'Net Sales' are calculated. This contradicts Section 3.2, which mandates documentation detailing the payment calculation, as the base calculation is no longer precisely defined, thus creating a contradiction on how the payments are going to be made
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Payments to HDI shall be due monthly within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar month and shall be accompanied by documentation reasonably detailing the calculation of the payment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific percentage and the minimum sales amount needed before HDI gets paid, along with the explicit definition of Net Sales. The contradicted text states that the payments will have documentation detailing the payment calculation. By removing the base payment calculation in Section 3.1, it is now impossible to provide documentation reasonably detailing the calculation of the payment because the amount is no longer concrete, as specified in Section 3.2, resulting in a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 Termination Payment. (a) In the event the Company terminates this Agreement, the Company shall pay to HDI a termination payment, (the "Termination Payment") the amount of which shall be an amount equal to the aggregate Net Sales for the twelve (12) months preceding the Termination Date less $800,000, the balance of which shall be divided by two. (b) In the event that the amount of the Termination -4- Payment is determined pursuant to this Section 4.2 to be less than or equal to zero, then no Termination Payment shall be due to HDI nor shall HDI be required to make any termination payment to the Company if the amount is determined to be less than zero. (c) The Termination Payment, if any, shall be payable by the Company in eight (8) equal payments to be made quarterly, commencing thirty (30) days after the termination date.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 Termination Payment. (a) In the event the Company terminates this Agreement, the Company shall pay to HDI a termination payment, (the "Termination Payment"). (c) The Termination Payment, if any, shall be payable by the Company following termination date.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the calculation method for the Termination Payment, the contract no longer defines how this payment will be determined. The removal of the clause specifying how to handle Termination Payment amounts less than zero creates uncertainty. Further omitting the schedule specifying payments to be made in eight equal payments to be made quarterly makes contradicting on how payments will be made following the termination date, making the payment terms ambiguous. This will therefore create an internal contradiction, as is expected the payment terms to be clearly outlined in the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Termination Payment. (a) In the event the Company terminates this Agreement, the Company shall pay to HDI a termination payment, (the "Termination Payment"). (c) The Termination Payment, if any, shall be payable by the Company following termination date.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a clear calculation method for the Termination Payment, including handling scenarios where the calculated amount is less than zero. The changed text removes this calculation method, creating ambiguity about how the Termination Payment is determined. The original text also outlined a specific payment schedule (eight equal quarterly payments), while the changed text only states that the payment will be made 'following termination date', omitting payment terms. Because of this omission, the altered language introduces a contradiction with the expectation that payment terms will be clearly defined in the original document, making the altered payment terms ambiguous.
